items.title: 'Rewarding loyalists,' punishing critics: How Trump's Treasury sanctions foreigners
items.summary: Spain's Prime Minister called U.S. strikes against Iran "unjustified." When other foreigners in power have used similar language against the U.S. or Israel, they were sanctioned by the Treasury.

items.title: How the energy crisis fueled by the war in Iran is affecting Asia
items.summary: Asia is feeling the energy shock of the war on Iran. NPR's A Martinez talks to Sam Reynolds of the Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis.

items.title: Morning news brief
items.summary: Israel's ground moves in Lebanon raise pressure on Western countries, Europeans leaders reject Trump's demand to help with the Strait of Hormuz, blackout leaves millions without power in Cuba.

items.title: Millions of migrants in the Middle East caught in the middle of the war in Iran
items.summary: As the war in Iran continues, millions of migrant workers in the Gulf are caught up in the ongoing conflict, adding to their already vulnerable existence.

items.title: Trump says Europeans should help secure the Strait of Hormuz. So far, they've refused
items.summary: European leaders are rejecting President Trump's demand they send military assets to the Middle East to help open the Strait of Hormuz, which is being obstructed by Iran.

items.title: How NATO countries are reacting to Trump's request for help with the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: NPR's A Martinez asks Ian Lesser of the German Marshall Fund of the United States how NATO regards President Trump's request for help to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Island-wide blackout knocks out power to millions in Cuba amid ongoing energy crisis
items.summary: Cuba plunges into darkness as nationwide blackouts hits over 10 million people on the island, worsening an energy crisis fueled by oil shortages and U.S. sanctions.

items.title: Afghanistan says 400 people killed in Pakistan strike on Kabul hospital
items.summary: Afghanistan has accused Pakistan of targeting a hospital for drug users in the Afghan capital with an airstrike, marking a dramatic escalation of a conflict that began late last month. Pakistan has dismissed the accusation.

items.title: Supreme Court to hear expedited arguments on protected status for migrants
items.summary: The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.

items.title: What are Trump's options in Iran as oil stops flowing and allies resist joining war?
items.summary: Iran has effectively shut down the flow of oil from the Gulf region. President Trump hasn't offered a clear solution. Trump is calling for allies to assist the U.S. military in removing the Iranian threat, and many of the allies are resisting.
